title: Reviewer Verdict
description: Machine-readable verdict block produced by a reviewer turn. Must be embedded as a fenced ```json block in the Verification section of the five-part turn body. All properties are required by OpenAI strict response_format; fields not relevant to the current mode MUST be emitted as null. convergence_status/next_action_hint/evidence_delta_vs_prior_round/evidence_delta_notes: convergence-loop mode only (emit null otherwise). scope_violation/dissenting_concerns: reviewer-aggregator mode only (emit null otherwise).

acceptance.description: One entry per acceptance criterion from GOAL.md.

criterion.description: Verbatim text of the acceptance criterion from GOAL.md.

verdict.description: COVERED = fully satisfied with evidence; PARTIAL = partly done; MISSING = not done; VERIFICATION-NOT-EVIDENCED = claimed done but output not captured.

evidence.description: File path + line range, command + output excerpt, or explanation of absence.

status.description: OK = no out-of-scope writes; VIOLATION = something outside in-scope paths was touched.

details.description: Empty string when OK. Describe what was touched out of scope when VIOLATION.

blocking_issues.description: Bugs, security issues, perf concerns, or scope violations. Empty array if none.

severity.description: BLOCKER = must fix before accept; MAJOR = should fix; MINOR = nice to fix.

file.description: Absolute file path + line range, e.g. /repo/src/foo.py:42-55. Use 'n/a' for cross-cutting issues.

suggested_fix.description: One-sentence proposed resolution; no patch text.

convergence_status.description: Convergence-loop mode only; emit null in one-shot reviewer and reviewer-aggregator modes. Reviewer's verdict on whether this round made measurable progress toward the goal versus the prior round.

next_action_hint.description: Convergence-loop mode only; emit null otherwise. Short directive to the parent loop controller, e.g. 'executor-rerun', 'planner-revise', 'switch-model', 'branch', 'escalate-to-user', 'accept-and-stop'.

evidence_delta_vs_prior_round.description: Convergence-loop mode only; emit null otherwise. Discrete classification of how this round's evidence differs from the prior round's reviewer verdict. 'new' = materially fresh evidence (artifact / failure shape / metric); 'partial' = some movement but key gaps unchanged; 'none' = no new evidence at all (loop-controller fast-fail signal — see roundtable-goal Phase 5); 'n/a-first-round' = no prior round to compare against. Use evidence_delta_notes for free-form context.

evidence_delta_notes.description: Convergence-loop mode only; emit null otherwise. Free-form one-paragraph note describing what specifically changed (or did not) versus the prior round's verdict.

scope_violation.description: Reviewer-aggregator mode only; emit null in reviewer and devils-advocate modes. Result of comparing executor-touched paths against GOAL.md 'In-scope paths'. When detected == true the aggregator's verdict MUST be 'revise' regardless of other signals.

detected.description: True iff git diff --stat against the thread-base-sha touched any path outside GOAL.md In-scope paths.

paths.description: Offending paths (relative to repo root). Empty when detected == false.

notes.description: One-sentence aggregator note (e.g. command run, why a touched path is out of scope, suggested revert target). Empty string when detected == false.

dissenting_concerns.description: Reviewer-aggregator mode only; emit null in reviewer and devils-advocate modes. Minority dissent that the aggregator disagrees with but preserves for the record. Each entry cites which reviewer raised it and why the aggregator did not promote it to blocking_issues.

source_reviewer.description: Actor/alias of the reviewer who raised this concern.

aggregator_rationale.description: Why the aggregator did not promote this to a blocking issue.
